Um, and then when you're failing and struggling it's easy to think you suck and think that, um, you know, you don't know what you're doing. First of all, it's not linear as well. Like there's times when you go up and down even after you've made money, right? But no matter what, you still have to to be consistent and keep your approach the same. Like don't switch up your approach just because things went bad and if things if things go good, don't switch up your approach then either because whatever got you there, you need to keep doing it. Um, and and in YouTube a lot of people get comfortable and that's when things can start to slip. So, um, in general it's just like just just try to remain consistent no matter what through through good periods, bad periods, have the same mindset. And I'd say the the mindset that you need is like an optimist that has a semi-long-term outlook. You want short-term confirmation, but your outlook should be long-term. Um, thinking about where you where you're going to be at in several months from now to years instead of like next week because like week-to-week YouTube is like not really predictable, but over the course of several months it's much more predictable. So, that's it for me, I think. Just like being consistent for like months on end if you can. Uh, that that's like a major major thing. Yeah, that's a golden piece of advice. I love it. All right. Thank you everyone for watching. Thank you, Ron, for showing up and sharing all of that sauce with us and uh, I'll see you guys in the next one.
